stdout:
OBSERVATIONS:
- Image 1, category 6 (noise/optics): the flat green midtones show no visible sensor grain at all — even in a JPEG-compressed macro shot, real cameras leave photon/read noise as fine speckling; here the tonal transitions are perfectly smooth with only faint quantization banding, which reads as a rendered gradient rather than a photographed surface.
- Image 1, category 2 (texture): there is no waviness, ripple, or mottling anywhere in the frame — just a single smooth elliptical light-to-dark gradient. Real rolled/hand-poured glass retains organic low-frequency thickness variation even when defocused; this surface is texture-free.
- Image 1, category 1 (reflection): the bright blob in the upper-left has a perfectly soft, structureless radial falloff with no hint of a light-source shape, window mullion, or room object — consistent with a procedural fresnel/highlight falloff rather than an actual bounced reflection of a real environment.
- Image 1, category 3 (color depth): the color shifts as one continuous smooth gradient rather than the blotchy, piece-to-piece thickness variation seen in real cathedral glass — looks like a flat tint modulated by a single light falloff, not physically varying material.
- Image 2, category 2 (texture): the blue swirled border shows genuine irregular streak widths and directions following the swirl pattern, and the green leaf glass has organic mottled patches — consistent with real rolled/streaky art glass.
- Image 2, category 6 (noise/optics): the foreground plant leaf shows real depth-of-field falloff against the panel, and the dark background in the backlit shot shows visible shadow noise/grain consistent with a real low-light camera exposure.
- Image 2, category 5 (lighting): the backlit half shows plausible single-source falloff (brighter center-top, darker corners) and color bleed onto the surrounding dark room, consistent with an actual lamp behind the panel.

VERDICT: Image 1 synthetic -- it lacks any sensor noise or organic glass texture and shows only a single structureless procedural-looking highlight gradient, while image 2 exhibits real camera grain, DOF falloff, and organic streak/mottle texture consistent with a genuine photograph.
